title: BlackBerry Earnings Review and 2026 Outlook
text: BlackBerry reported Q1 FY2026 revenue of approximately $173M, representing a 7% year-over-year decline but beating expectations. The company delivered adjusted EPS of roughly $0.03 versus breakeven expectations. Management attributed the upside to better cost control and operating leverage, particularly within the Secure Communications segment. The company operates through two primary divisions: Secure Communications (cybersecurity and endpoint security solutions) and IoT (QNX software for automotive systems). BlackBerry's software runs in over 235 million vehicles globally. The earnings demonstrated improving profitability through operational discipline, yet revealed a persistent weakness: top-line growth remains muted due to long production cycles in the IoT segment. Management guided for flat to slightly declining revenue near-term while emphasizing continued margin expansion. BlackBerry faces a critical challenge: converting profitability gains into sustainable revenue growth. The company's transformation from smartphone manufacturer to enterprise software provider continues, but analyst reactions were cautiously positive on cost discipline while concerns persisted regarding revenue stagnation. The stock remains technically weak, showing lower highs and weak momentum without breaking above key resistance levels.
